title: interval-glsl parity corpus (Tycho → CE)
purpose: Representative implicit-curve field functions f(x,y) that Tycho renders, plus targeted edge cases, for the interval-glsl ↔ interval-js parity harness (INTERVAL_GLSL_PLAN.md §6, §9). Each f defines a curve as its zero set f=0; the renderer's question per cell is 'can f be 0 in this box?' (lo ≤ 0 ≤ hi).
howToUse: Parse each `latex` with `ce.parse(latex, { strict: false })`. Compile to both `interval-glsl` and `interval-js`. Over a grid of input boxes covering `viewport`, assert the GLSL interval CONTAINS the interval-js interval (over-approximation OK; under-approximation = bug → a missed crossing). All 25 entries are verified to compile on Tycho's `interval-js` path; `kindsObserved` is what interval-js actually returned over representative boxes.

parityProperty.containment: GLSL_result ⊇ interval-js_result over every box. Under-approximation is the only unsound direction.
parityProperty.emptyIsLoadBearing: Because `lo > hi` encodes `empty` (→ exclude), the critical assertion is GPU-empty ⇒ CPU-empty. A GPU `empty` cell where interval-js is non-empty is a containment violation (∅ cannot contain a non-empty set), i.e. a false exclude → missed crossing. Treat `empty` as ∅ in the containment check.
parityProperty.singularMapsToEntire: interval-js returns `kind:'singular'` at a pole; the GPU contract returns `entire` there (div spanning zero). `entire ⊇ singular`, so this is sound — both mean 'cannot exclude', and Tycho's CPU break-classifier (not the GPU) turns the pole into an asymptote break.

kindsLegend.empty: domain undefined over the whole box (e.g. sqrt of a fully-negative interval) → no curve → safe to exclude. GPU encodes as lo>hi.
kindsLegend.singular: pole / div-spanning-zero (interval-js tag). GPU contract returns `entire` here.

phases.1: arithmetic only: Add, Negate, Multiply, Divide (+ number literals). Runnable after Phase 1.
phases.2: + Power (square / pow-int / rational-pow), Abs, Sqrt, Ln/Exp/Log. Covers polynomial / rational / algebraic implicit curves — the high-value core.
phases.3: + trig & inverse-trig: Sin, Cos, Arcsin, … Until Phase 3, these route to CPU interval-js automatically via the `unsupported` field (ask B).

literalHeadsNote: Integer / Rational / Real heads are number literals handled by the `number → vec2(n,n)` hook, not functions to port; they are omitted from each entry's `requires`. A Power with a Rational exponent (e.g. x^{3/2}) is the even-denominator restricted-domain case and is called out in `notes`.

entries.title: Rectangular hyperbola xy = 1
entries.notes: Cleanest Phase-1 entry: mul of intervals straddling 0 (the four-product min/max path) with no Power.

entries.title: Reciprocal y = 1/x
entries.notes: A box straddling x=0 makes the denominator span 0. interval-js returns `singular`; GPU contract returns `entire` (must be WIDE, never narrow).

entries.title: Reciprocal field with a ring pole
exercises: pole on a curve (not a point)
entries.notes: Denominator x²+y²−0.25 crosses 0 on a whole circle, so many cells hit div-spanning-zero. GPU → entire there; CPU → singular.

entries.title: Astroid |x|^(2/3) + |y|^(2/3) = 1
entries.domain: all (abs guards the base — NOT restricted)
entries.notes: Contrast with pow-half: because the base is |x| ≥ 0, the rational exponent 2/3 is always defined → no `empty`. Confirms the abs-guard case must NOT spuriously go empty.

entries.title: Product of two non-monotonic factors
exercises: mul of two zero-spanning intervals (four-product min/max)
entries.notes: Both factors straddle 0 across much of the viewport — exercises the mul endpoint-product min/max in the regime where a naive 2-product would be wrong.

entries.title: Restricted domain: √x + √y = 1
exercises: sqrt clamp on a straddling box
entries.notes: Boxes fully in x<0 or y<0 must yield `empty` (GPU lo>hi). Boxes straddling x=0 clamp to [sqrt(max(lo,0)), sqrt(hi)]. This is the primary `empty`-propagation fixture.

entries.title: Restricted domain: ln x + ln y + 1 = 0
entries.notes: Exercises both `empty` (x≤0) and the −IV_INF sentinel clamp as x→0⁺ (ln→−∞ must clamp to −IV_INF, never materialize a real −inf).

entries.title: Even-denominator rational power: x^(3/2) + y = 1
exercises: rational power, even denominator → restricted domain → empty
entries.notes: The even-denominator rational-pow domain case §9 calls out. Boxes in x<0 → `empty`. Contrast astroid-true (abs-guarded, never empty).

entries.title: GLSL implicit lattice (trig)
exercises: sin/cos range over wide intervals (range reduction)
entries.notes: The headline trig showcase curve. High-frequency → boxes span multiple periods → stresses sin/cos interval range reduction the hardest.

entries.title: sin(x)·cos(y) = 0
exercises: grid of straight lines at multiples of π/2

entries.title: Inverse-trig domain: arcsin x + arcsin y = 0
entries.notes: interval-js clamped a straddling box to the valid sub-range (returned `interval`, not `empty`). A box fully outside [−1,1] should yield `empty`. Useful to confirm the GPU and CPU agree on inverse-trig domain handling once Phase 3 lands.
